title: The last transformer gradient nodes cross to the four-kernel floor — gelu' and softmax'/layernorm' four-way
advances: Closes the transformer block's backward pass at the NODE level on all four kernels. Prior runs lifted the affine node (transformer-backprop, 127) and the attention-core node (transformer-attention-backward, 31) to four-way, but the two REMAINING gradient nodes — backprop THROUGH the gelu nonlinearity, and the softmax'/layernorm' gradients proven by their conservation laws — lived in their own bands (transformer-backprop-ffn, transformer-backprop-softmax-ln) that had only ever run three-way (Go/Rust/TS). Lifting both makes EVERY individual gradient node of the transformer block a gated four-kernel invariant on every suite run: affine (127) + gelu (63) + softmax+layernorm (31) + attention-core (31). The backward floor at the node level now matches the four-way forward floor (transformer-numerics 4095, transformer-block 511) — the floor under emitting the trainable block to Metal, the training half of the block 3m (PR #3292) just proved running forward on the M4 GPU.
device: Apple M4 Max (40-core GPU), arm64 Darwin 26.3.1 — proof is host-independent (pure fp64 arithmetic bands)

bands_lifted.what: backprop composes THROUGH the gelu nonlinearity. The FFN sub-block y = W2*gelu(W1*x + b1) + b2 is the affine vjp twice with ONE new node between them: the elementwise gelu derivative. gelu' is composed from tn-tanh (the SAME z and constants tn-gelu uses), so it is exact-bit-deterministic across kernels. A 2->2->2 MLP trains from a non-trivial init (so the chain through gelu is genuinely exercised), lr=0.1 fp64, loss falls toward 0.
bands_lifted.claims: 1: gelu'(0)=0.5 exactly (the bit-anchor). 2: gelu'(1)=1.082964 (gelu' is NOT identity — the nonlinearity modulates the gradient). 4: gelu'(-0.75)=0.001062 (the dip near gelu's minimum, the strange edge). 8: the FFN's unfit start carries positive loss. 16: ONE SGD step through the gelu chain lowers loss (learning happened through the nonlinearity). 32: thirty steps lower it further (convergence through the nonlinearity). => 63.
bands_lifted.preludes: form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/transformer-numerics.fk form-stdlib/transformer-block.fk form-stdlib/transformer-backprop.fk (all already four-way)

bands_lifted.what: the two remaining gradient nodes, proven by their CONSERVATION LAWS rather than by tolerance. softmax' dz = s (.) (g - <g,s>) has Sum dz = 0 (softmax conserves probability mass — exact). layernorm' dx = (1/sigma)[g - mean(g) - y*mean(g (.) y)] has Sum dx = 0 (orthogonal to the mean direction — exact, since Sum y = 0) and <dx,y> ~ 0 (orthogonal to scale — exact only in infinite precision; here ~4e-6 because the body's sigma carries eps and a 50-step Newton sqrt, so y is not exactly unit-variance).
bands_lifted.claims: 1: softmax' conserves mass Sum dz = 0 (exact). 2: softmax' pinned dz[0] -> 224483 (x1e6). 4: layernorm' mean-orthogonal Sum dx = 0 (exact). 8: layernorm' pinned dx[0] -> 571545 (x1e6). 16: layernorm' scale-orthogonal to ~4e-6 (|<dx,y>*1e6| <= 10, the eps/Newton reason named honestly). => 31.
bands_lifted.preludes: form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/transformer-numerics.fk form-stdlib/transformer-block.fk form-stdlib/transformer-backprop.fk (all already four-way)

proof: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/transformer-numerics.fk form-stdlib/transformer-block.fk form-stdlib/transformer-backprop.fk form-stdlib/tests/transformer-backprop-ffn-band.fk  ->  '-> 63', 'fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way', '1 ok, 0 divergent'. Same for transformer-backprop-softmax-ln-band.fk -> '-> 31', four-way, 0 divergent.
verdict: FOUR-WAY (ffn 63, softmax-ln 31), 0 divergent — Go, Rust, TypeScript, and the emitted fkwu walker all reproduce the gelu-chain backward (gelu'(0)=0.5, gelu'(1)=1.082964, gelu'(-0.75)=0.001062, FFN training through the nonlinearity) and the softmax'/layernorm' gradients by their exact conservation laws, bit-for-bit at 1e-6.
manifest: registered in form/fourth-arm-bands.txt as 'transformer-backprop-ffn fks 63' and 'transformer-backprop-softmax-ln fks 31', placed in the transformer family directly after 'transformer-attention-backward fks 31'.
reading: With these two bands, every INDIVIDUAL gradient node of transformer-block.fk's pre-LN block is four-kernel proven: affine (transformer-backprop 127), gelu (this run, 63), softmax+layernorm (this run, 31), attention-core (transformer-attention-backward 31). Combined with the forward numerics (transformer-numerics 4095) and forward block (transformer-block 511), and the load-path dequant math (Q4_K 4095, Q6_K 4095, f16-decode 4095), the weight load AND the block forward+backward node math all live on the four-way floor — the four-kernel-proven algorithm under the jte-*-train-msl Metal trainers already running on the M4 GPU.

honest_gap.what: The end-to-end assembled-stack TRAINING bands stay 3-kernel-only: transformer-block-backward (two FFN-residual blocks + 150-step SGD loop, verdict 31) and transformer-block-assembly (heterogeneous typed-layer stack + string-tag dispatch + 50-step recursion, verdict 63). Go=Rust=TS agree on both.
honest_gap.kind: fkwu TABLE-CAPACITY wall — NOT a value divergence and NOT a missing op family. fkwu Segmentation-faults on the largest composed pre-flattened table before producing any answer; the three native walkers agree. The constituent gradient nodes all cross four-way in isolation (this run + prior), so the wall is table SIZE, not the math.
honest_gap.resolution: An fkwu walker/emitter robustness fix (raise recursion/table capacity), orthogonal to the proven recipes — when the capacity lifts, both assembled bands should cross with no recipe change. Documented as the 'table-capacity wall' standing-wall category in form/fourth-arm-bands.txt; flagged for Urs in self_time_calibration.md. NOT shipped in the manifest (honest 3-kernel-only), NOT dressed as a gap-that-could-be-a-divergence.

reproduce: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/transformer-numerics.fk form-stdlib/transformer-block.fk form-stdlib/transformer-backprop.fk form-stdlib/tests/transformer-backprop-ffn-band.fk  (four-way 63, 0 divergent); same with transformer-backprop-softmax-ln-band.fk (four-way 31). Run each band individually: rapid back-to-back validate.sh calls share a temp dir and can report a spurious divergence; the canonical suite isolates them.
free_brick_note: Read-the-body again (the fifth time in this autonomous walk): the recipes (tbp-ffn-* / gelu' / softmax' / layernorm' vjp in transformer-backprop.fk) and their reference-pinned bands already existed three-way. The brick was two manifest rows + confirming fkwu carries the op families (pure fp64 arithmetic + small training recursions — small tables, under the capacity wall). Design-cost ~nil, proof = seconds, because the body had already built the hard part. The one band that did NOT cross (block-backward) hit the table-capacity wall and is named honestly, never buried.
